input_content:
Vaping left black holes in my teeth, says woman with £120-a-month habit

Vaping left black holes in my teeth, says woman with £120-a-month habit  BBC

sections.Press Release:
**HEADLINE:** Vaping Industry Responds to Isolated Case: "Don't Let One Story Overshadow Four Million Success Stories"

**SUBHEADLINE:** Riot Labs calls for perspective as misleading coverage threatens to undermine harm reduction progress that has helped millions quit smoking

**DATELINE:** [DATE], London

A leading British vape manufacturer has urged the public not to lose sight of the bigger picture after a BBC report highlighted one woman's negative experience with vaping, warning that isolated cases risk overshadowing the millions of success stories that don't make headlines.

The BBC story follows a pattern of coverage that amplifies individual negative experiences while ignoring the overwhelming scientific consensus that vaping has helped over four million adults in the UK quit smoking — the leading preventable cause of death in the country.

David Donaghy, Head of Brand & Marketing at Riot Labs, said: "Every negative experience deserves to be taken seriously, but we can't let one story eclipse the four million people who've used vaping to escape cigarettes. The woman in this story was spending £120 a month — that's not normal vaping, that's excessive consumption that would be harmful with any product. Most people use vaping as a temporary bridge away from smoking, not a permanent £1,400-a-year habit."

Public Health England's landmark research established that vaping is at least 95% less harmful than smoking, yet media coverage often presents vaping and smoking as equivalent risks. This creates confusion among the 6.4 million adults who still smoke and are considering switching to a less harmful alternative.

"We're not claiming vaping is risk-free," Donaghy added. "But when 75,000 people die from smoking every year in the UK, we need to keep this in perspective. The biggest dental health risk facing smokers isn't black holes from excessive vaping — it's the cancer, gum disease and tooth loss that comes from continuing to smoke cigarettes."

Riot Labs manufactures its products in the UK and operates Riot Rehab, a smoking cessation initiative designed to help people transition away from both cigarettes and vaping entirely. The company has consistently advocated for age-gated access and responsible consumption messaging across the industry.

**About Riot Labs**
Riot Labs is a British vaping company on a mission to help people quit smoking for good. Founded and headquartered in the UK, Riot manufactures its e-liquids through Fantasia Flavour House, its in-house flavour development operation. The company's portfolio includes Riot Bar Edtn, RIOT CONNEX and KURO. Through its Riot Activist campaigning arm, Riot fights misinformation and disproportionate regulation that would harm adult smokers' right to switch to less harmful alternatives. Riot is a member of the IBVTA and UKVIA. For more information visit riotlabs.com.

**Media contact:** [MEDIA CONTACT PLACEHOLDER]

---
sections.Journalist Pitch Email:
**Subject:** Response available: Why one vaping horror story doesn't change the science on harm reduction

Hi [JOURNALIST NAME],

Your readers deserve context on today's BBC vaping story that's already being shared widely on social media.

The piece highlights one woman's £120-a-month vaping habit and resulting dental issues — but doesn't mention that Public Health England research shows vaping has helped over four million adults quit smoking, the UK's leading preventable cause of death.

We can offer:
• David Donaghy, Head of Brand & Marketing at Riot Labs, available for immediate comment
• Data on normal vaping consumption vs the extreme case highlighted
• Context on dental health risks: vaping vs continued smoking
• Examples from Riot's smoking cessation work with people transitioning off cigarettes entirely

This isn't about defending excessive consumption — it's about ensuring your readers understand the relative risks when 6.4 million adults are still smoking.

David is available by phone until 6pm today or first thing tomorrow.

Best regards,
[NAME]

---
sections.LinkedIn Post:
The BBC ran a story today about a woman who developed dental issues from a £120-a-month vaping habit. 

While every negative experience deserves attention, we can't let one extreme case overshadow the bigger picture: over four million adults in the UK have used vaping to quit smoking.

£120 a month isn't normal vaping consumption — it's excessive use that would be harmful with any product. Most people use vaping as a temporary bridge away from cigarettes, not a £1,400-a-year permanent habit.

The science remains clear: vaping is at least 95% less harmful than smoking. When 75,000 people die from smoking-related illness every year, perspective matters.

We're not claiming vaping is risk-free. But fear-driven coverage that treats vaping and smoking as equivalent risks does a disservice to the 6.4 million adults still trying to quit cigarettes.

What are your thoughts on how media coverage affects public health decisions?

**Suggested imagery:** Quote card featuring the statistic "4 million UK adults have used vaping to quit smoking" with the Riot logo, designed in Riot's brand colours with clean, professional typography.

---
sections.Retailer WhatsApp Comms:
**NOT APPLICABLE** — This story doesn't directly impact trade operations, product availability, or require immediate retailer action. Retailers may see increased customer questions, but this doesn't warrant urgent WhatsApp comms.

---
sections.Consumer Social Media Comms:
Stories like today's BBC piece can be scary. But let's keep some perspective 📊

One person's £120/month habit ≠ normal vaping
4 million successful quitters > one negative story
95% less harmful than smoking = the science hasn't changed

We're not saying vaping is perfect. We're saying it's a hell of a lot better than smoking 🚭

Questions about quitting smoking OR vaping? Our Riot Rehab team can help.

#HarmReduction #QuitSmoking #VapingFacts #RiotRehab

**Suggested hashtags:** #HarmReduction #QuitSmoking #VapingFacts #RiotRehab #ScienceNotScare #PerspectiveMatters

**Suggested visual direction:** Split-screen graphic showing "1 negative story vs 4 million success stories" with simple, clear statistics and Riot branding. Keep it factual, not dismissive.

---
sections.Internal Briefing: **NOT APPLICABLE** — While this story may generate some customer conversations, it doesn't require immediate internal briefing. The BBC coverage is isolated rather than indicating a broader crisis that would affect day-to-day operations or sales conversations.
